sections.paragraph: ὄντως [fr. ὤν, ptc. of εἰμί] adv. ‘in every sense of the word’, unquestionably, really Mk 11:32; Lk 23:47; 24:34; J 8:36; 1 Cor 14:25; Gal 3:21; actually 2 Pt 2:18 v.l. Functioning adjectively real: of widows who are in need 1 Ti 5:3, 5, 16; of life in the highest sense 6:19; but in these pass., where ὄν. is used between art. and noun, the phrases may be rendered ‘she who is (those who are) really widow(s)...’, ‘that which is really life’.
